name: Epic E-2 VPC Networking
description: Implement Epic E-2: Networking (VPC) for the petclinic-platform infrastructure repo. Build a reusable Terraform VPC module with all-public subnets, Internet Gateway, route tables, required EKS subnet tags, and baseline security groups. Wire the module into the dev and prod environment root modules using the CIDRs and AZs defined in docs/technical-spec.md.

goal.answer: Implement Epic E-2: Networking (VPC) for the petclinic-platform infrastructure repo. Build a reusable Terraform VPC module with all-public subnets, Internet Gateway, route tables, required EKS subnet tags, and baseline security groups. Wire the module into the dev and prod environment root modules using the CIDRs and AZs defined in docs/technical-spec.md.

criteria.answer:
terraform/modules/vpc/ contains a working VPC module with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf
VPC module creates a VPC with DNS support and hostnames enabled
VPC module creates 2 public subnets across 2 AZs with map_public_ip_on_launch=true and required EKS tags
VPC module creates and attaches an Internet Gateway
VPC module creates a public route table with 0.0.0.0/0 -> IGW
VPC module creates baseline security groups: EKS cluster, EKS node, RDS, ALB
terraform/environments/dev/main.tf calls the VPC module with dev CIDRs and outputs required IDs
terraform/environments/prod/main.tf calls the VPC module with prod CIDRs and outputs required IDs
terraform/environments/dev/terraform.tfvars and prod/terraform.tfvars define environment-specific CIDRs and AZs
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/dev and terraform/environments/prod
terraform fmt -recursive -check terraform/ passes

constraints.answer: Use existing Terraform project conventions (provider ~> 5.0, required_version >= 1.6.0, tags Project/Environment/ManagedBy), All-public subnet design: no NAT Gateway, no private subnets, no VPC endpoints (per ADR-0001), CIDRs must match docs/technical-spec.md: dev 10.0.0.0/16, prod 10.1.0.0/16, Do not modify the application repo (spring-petclinic-microservices)

phases.answer: Implement VPC module, Wire VPC into environments, Validate VPC configuration

assumptions.answer: AWS region us-east-2 is used for both environments; At least 2 availability zones are available in us-east-2; The S3 backend from E-1 is bootstrapped and terraform init has been run in both environments; Baseline security groups are created now; rules will be refined in later epics (EKS, RDS, ALB)

phases.name: Implement VPC module
phases.goal: Create the reusable VPC module with subnets, IGW, route tables, and security groups

steps.description: Create terraform/modules/vpc/versions.tf with AWS provider constraint ~> 5.0
Create terraform/modules/vpc/variables.tf with project, environment, vpc_cidr, public_subnet_cidrs, availability_zones, tags

verification.command: grep -q 'variable "vpc_cidr"' terraform/modules/vpc/variables.tf && grep -q 'variable "public_subnet_cidrs"' terraform/modules/vpc/variables.tf && grep -q 'variable "availability_zones"' terraform/modules/vpc/variables.tf

steps.description: Create terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf with VPC, subnets, IGW, route table, and security groups

verification.command: grep -q 'aws_vpc' terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf && grep -q 'aws_subnet' terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf && grep -q 'aws_internet_gateway' terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf && grep -q 'aws_route_table' terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf && grep -q 'aws_security_group' terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf

steps.description: Create terraform/modules/vpc/outputs.tf with vpc_id, public_subnet_ids, eks_cluster_sg_id, eks_node_sg_id, rds_sg_id, alb_sg_id

verification.command: grep -q 'output "vpc_id"' terraform/modules/vpc/outputs.tf && grep -q 'output "public_subnet_ids"' terraform/modules/vpc/outputs.tf && grep -q 'output "eks_cluster_sg_id"' terraform/modules/vpc/outputs.tf && grep -q 'output "alb_sg_id"' terraform/modules/vpc/outputs.tf

phases.summary: Phase 1 completed: created the reusable VPC module under terraform/modules/vpc/ with versions.tf, variables.tf, main.tf, and outputs.tf. The module defines a VPC with DNS support/hostnames, two public subnets with EKS tags, an Internet Gateway, a public route table with 0.0.0.0/0 -> IGW, and baseline security groups for EKS cluster, EKS node, RDS, and ALB.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Wire VPC into environments
phases.goal: Call the VPC module from dev and prod root modules and set environment-specific values

steps.description: Update terraform/environments/dev/main.tf to call vpc module with dev CIDRs and AZs
Update terraform/environments/prod/main.tf to call vpc module with prod CIDRs and AZs

phases.summary: Phase 2 completed: VPC module is wired into both dev and prod root modules and tfvars files contain required VPC variables.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Validate VPC configuration
phases.goal: Ensure Terraform configuration is formatted and valid in both environments
phases.summary: Phase 3 completed: Terraform formatting check passed, and terraform validate passed in both dev and prod environments.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

successCriteria: terraform/modules/vpc/ contains a working VPC module with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf
VPC module creates a VPC with DNS support and hostnames enabled
VPC module creates 2 public subnets across 2 AZs with map_public_ip_on_launch=true and required EKS tags
VPC module creates and attaches an Internet Gateway
VPC module creates a public route table with 0.0.0.0/0 -> IGW
VPC module creates baseline security groups: EKS cluster, EKS node, RDS, ALB
terraform/environments/dev/main.tf calls the VPC module with dev CIDRs and outputs required IDs
terraform/environments/prod/main.tf calls the VPC module with prod CIDRs and outputs required IDs
terraform/environments/dev/terraform.tfvars and prod/terraform.tfvars define environment-specific CIDRs and AZs
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/dev and terraform/environments/prod
constraints: Use existing Terraform project conventions (provider ~> 5.0, required_version >= 1.6.0, tags Project/Environment/ManagedBy)
All-public subnet design: no NAT Gateway, no private subnets, no VPC endpoints (per ADR-0001)
CIDRs must match docs/technical-spec.md: dev 10.0.0.0/16, prod 10.1.0.0/16

grade.rationale: Although all phase and ferment-scope gates claim pass, the visible diff substantiates only documentation/script region-string changes and does not show the required terraform/modules/vpc files, environment root modules, or .tfvars files (they are hidden in truncated untracked files). Gate rationales mention 'grep assertions' and 'terraform commands' without citing specific file paths, commands, or outputs, making verification thin under a pessimistic read.
